NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David. Deliver me, O LORD, from evil men; Preserve me from violent men,
NKJV.2: Who plan evil things in their hearts; They continually gather together for war.
NKJV.3: They sharpen their tongues like a serpent; The poison of asps is under their lips. Selah
NKJV.4: Keep me, O LORD, from the hands of the wicked; Preserve me from violent men, Who have purposed to make my steps stumble.
NKJV.5: The proud have hidden a snare for me, and cords; They have spread a net by the wayside; They have set traps for me. Selah
NKJV.6: I said to the LORD: “You are my God; Hear the voice of my supplications, O LORD.
NKJV.7: O GOD the Lord, the strength of my salvation, You have covered my head in the day of battle.
NKJV.8: Do not grant, O LORD, the desires of the wicked; Do not further his wicked scheme, Lest they be exalted. Selah
NKJV.9: “As for the head of those who surround me, Let the evil of their lips cover them;
NKJV.10: Let burning coals fall upon them; Let them be cast into the fire, Into deep pits, that they rise not up again.
NKJV.11: Let not a slanderer be established in the earth; Let evil hunt the violent man to overthrow him.”
NKJV.12: I know that the LORD will maintain The cause of the afflicted, And justice for the poor.
NKJV.13: Surely the righteous shall give thanks to Your name; The upright shall dwell in Your presence.

KJV.5: The proud<S>1343</S> have hid<S>2934</S> a snare<S>6341</S> for me, and cords<S>2256</S>; they have spread<S>6566</S> a net<S>7568</S> by the wayside<S>3027</S> <S>4570</S>; they have set<S>7896</S> gins<S>4170</S> for me. Selah<S>5542</S>.
KJV.6: I said<S>559</S> unto the LORD<S>3068</S>, Thou art my God<S>410</S>: hear<S>238</S> the voice<S>6963</S> of my supplications<S>8469</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>.
KJV.7: O GOD<S>3069</S> the Lord<S>136</S>, the strength<S>5797</S> of my salvation<S>3444</S>, thou hast covered<S>5526</S> my head<S>7218</S> in the day<S>3117</S> of battle<S>5402</S>.
KJV.8: Grant<S>5414</S> not, O LORD<S>3068</S>, the desires<S>3970</S> of the wicked<S>7563</S>: further<S>6329</S> not his wicked device<S>2162</S>; lest they exalt<S>7311</S> themselves. Selah<S>5542</S>. <sup>lest: or, let them not be exalted</sup>
KJV.9: As for the head<S>7218</S> of those that compass me about<S>4524</S>, let the mischief<S>5999</S> of their own lips<S>8193</S> cover<S>3680</S> them.

KJV.11: Let not an evil speaker<S>376</S> <S>3956</S> be established<S>3559</S> in the earth<S>776</S>: evil<S>7451</S> shall hunt<S>6679</S> the violent<S>2555</S> man<S>376</S> to overthrow<S>4073</S> him. <sup>an: Heb. a man of tongue: or an evil speaker, a wicked man of violence be established in the earth: let him be hunted to his overthrow</sup>
KJV.12: I know<S>3045</S> that the LORD<S>3068</S> will maintain<S>6213</S> the cause<S>1779</S> of the afflicted<S>6041</S>, and the right<S>4941</S> of the poor<S>34</S>.

ESV.1: Deliver me, O LORD, from evil men; preserve me from violent men,
ESV.2: who plan evil things in their heart and stir up wars continually.
ESV.3: They make their tongue sharp as a serpent's, and under their lips is the venom of asps. Selah
ESV.4: Guard me, O LORD, from the hands of the wicked; preserve me from violent men, who have planned to trip up my feet.
ESV.5: The arrogant have hidden a trap for me, and with cords they have spread a net; beside the way they have set snares for me. Selah
ESV.6: I say to the LORD, You are my God; give ear to the voice of my pleas for mercy, O LORD!
ESV.7: O LORD, my Lord, the strength of my salvation, you have covered my head in the day of battle.
ESV.8: Grant not, O LORD, the desires of the wicked; do not further their evil plot, or they will be exalted! Selah
ESV.9: As for the head of those who surround me, let the mischief of their lips overwhelm them!
ESV.10: Let burning coals fall upon them! Let them be cast into fire, into miry pits, no more to rise!
ESV.11: Let not the slanderer be established in the land; let evil hunt down the violent man speedily!
ESV.12: I know that the LORD will maintain the cause of the afflicted, and will execute justice for the needy.
ESV.13: Surely the righteous shall give thanks to your name; the upright shall dwell in your presence.

NASB.1: For the choir director. A Psalm of David. Rescue me, O LORD, from evil men; Preserve me from violent men
NASB.2: Who devise evil things in [their] hearts; They continually stir up wars.
NASB.3: They sharpen their tongues as a serpent; Poison of a viper is under their lips. Selah.
NASB.4: Keep me, O LORD, from the hands of the wicked; Preserve me from violent men Who have purposed to trip up my feet.
NASB.5: The proud have hidden a trap for me, and cords; They have spread a net by the wayside; They have set snares for me. Selah.
NASB.6: I said to the LORD, 'You are my God; Give ear, O LORD, to the voice of my supplications.
NASB.7: 'O GOD the Lord, the strength of my salvation, You have covered my head in the day of battle.
NASB.8: 'Do not grant, O LORD, the desires of the wicked; Do not promote his [evil] device, [that] they [not] be exalted. Selah.
NASB.9: 'As for the head of those who surround me, May the mischief of their lips cover them.
NASB.10: 'May burning coals fall upon them; May they be cast into the fire, Into deep pits from which they cannot rise.
NASB.11: 'May a slanderer not be established in the earth; May evil hunt the violent man speedily.'
NASB.12: I know that the LORD will maintain the cause of the afflicted And justice for the poor.
NASB.13: Surely the righteous will give thanks to Your name; The upright will dwell in Your presence.

NLT.1:
O LORD, rescue me from evil people.
Protect me from those who are violent,
NLT.2:
those who plot evil in their hearts
and stir up trouble all day long.
NLT.3:
Their tongues sting like a snake;
the venom of a viper drips from their lips. Interlude
NLT.4:
O LORD, keep me out of the hands of the wicked.
Protect me from those who are violent,
for they are plotting against me.
NLT.5:
The proud have set a trap to catch me;
they have stretched out a net;
they have placed traps all along the way. Interlude
NLT.6:
I said to the LORD, “You are my God!”
Listen, O LORD, to my cries for mercy!
NLT.7:
O Sovereign LORD, the strong one who rescued me,
you protected me on the day of battle.
NLT.8:
LORD, do not let evil people have their way.
Do not let their evil schemes succeed,
or they will become proud. Interlude
NLT.9:
Let my enemies be destroyed
by the very evil they have planned for me.
NLT.10:
Let burning coals fall down on their heads.
Let them be thrown into the fire
or into watery pits from which they can’t escape.
NLT.11:
Don’t let liars prosper here in our land.
Cause great disasters to fall on the violent.
NLT.12:
But I know the LORD will help those they persecute;
he will give justice to the poor.
NLT.13:
Surely righteous people are praising your name;
the godly will live in your presence.

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. A Psalm by David. Deliver me, Yahweh, from the evil man. Preserve me from the violent man;
WEB.2: those who devise mischief in their hearts. They continually gather themselves together for war.
WEB.3: They have sharpened their tongues like a serpent. Viper’s poison is under their lips. Selah.
WEB.4: Yahweh, keep me from the hands of the wicked. Preserve me from the violent men who have determined to trip my feet.
WEB.5: The proud have hidden a snare for me, they have spread the cords of a net by the path. They have set traps for me. Selah.
WEB.6: I said to Yahweh, “You are my God.” Listen to the cry of my petitions, Yahweh.
WEB.7: Yahweh, the Lord, the strength of my salvation, you have covered my head in the day of battle.
WEB.8: Yahweh, don’t grant the desires of the wicked. Don’t let their evil plans succeed, or they will become proud. Selah.
WEB.9: As for the head of those who surround me, let the mischief of their own lips cover them.
WEB.10: Let burning coals fall on them. Let them be thrown into the fire, into miry pits, from where they never rise.
WEB.11: An evil speaker won’t be established in the earth. Evil will hunt the violent man to overthrow him.
WEB.12: I know that Yahweh will maintain the cause of the afflicted, and justice for the needy.
WEB.13: Surely the righteous will give thanks to your name. The upright will dwell in your presence.

CSB17.1:
For the choir director. A psalm of David. [1] Rescue me, LORD, from evil men.
Keep me safe from violent men ⓙ
CSB17.2:
who plan evil in their hearts.
They stir up wars all day long. ⓚ
CSB17.3:
They make their tongues
as sharp as a snake’s bite;
viper’s venom is under their lips. ⓛ Selah
CSB17.4:
Protect me, LORD,
from the power of the wicked. ⓜ
Keep me safe from violent men
who plan to make me stumble. [1] ⓝ
CSB17.5:
The proud hide a trap with ropes for me;
they spread a net along the path
and set snares for me. ⓐ Selah
CSB17.6:
I say to the LORD, “You are my God.”
Listen, LORD, to my cry for help. ⓑ
CSB17.7:
LORD, my Lord, my strong Savior,
you shield my head on the day of battle. ⓒ
CSB17.8:
LORD, do not grant the desires of the wicked;
do not let them achieve their goals.
Otherwise, they will become proud. ⓓ Selah
CSB17.9:
When those who surround me rise up, [2]
may the trouble their lips cause overwhelm them. ⓔ

I [4] know that the LORD upholds

the upright will live in your presence. ⓘ

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David. Rescue me, O LORD, from evil men;
Protect me from violent men.
AMP.2:
They devise evil things in their hearts;
They continually [gather together and] stir up wars.
AMP.3:
They sharpen their tongues like a serpent's;
Poison of a viper is under their lips. Selah.
AMP.4:
Keep me, O LORD, from the hands of the wicked;
Protect me from violent men
Who intend to trip up my steps.
AMP.5:
The proud have hidden a trap for me, and cords;
They have spread a net by the wayside;
They have set traps for me. Selah.
AMP.6:
I said to the LORD, "You are my God;
Listen to the voice of my supplications, O LORD.
AMP.7:
"O GOD the Lord, the strength of my salvation,
You have covered my head in the day of battle.
AMP.8:
"Do not grant, O LORD, the desires of the wicked;
Do not further their evil device, that they not be exalted. Selah.
AMP.9:
"Those who surround me raise their heads;
May the mischief of their own lips come upon them.
AMP.10:
"Let burning coals fall upon them;
Let them be thrown into the fire,
Into deep [water] pits from which they cannot rise.
AMP.11:
"Do not let a slanderer be established in the earth;
Let evil quickly hunt the violent man [to overthrow him and stop his evil acts]."
AMP.12:
I know [with confidence] that the LORD will maintain the cause of the afflicted,
And [will secure] justice for the poor.
AMP.13:
Surely the righteous will give thanks to Your name;
The upright will dwell in Your presence.

MSG.1:
A David psalm
God, get me out of here, away from this evil;
protect me from these vicious people.
MSG.2:
All they do is think up new ways to be bad;
they spend their days plotting war games.
MSG.3:
They practice the sharp rhetoric of hate and hurt,
speak venomous words that maim and kill.
MSG.4:
God, keep me out of the clutch of these wicked ones,
protect me from these vicious people;
MSG.5:
Stuffed with self-importance, they plot ways to trip me up,
determined to bring me down.
These crooks invent traps to catch me
and do their best to incriminate me.
MSG.6:
I prayed, "God, you're my God!
Listen, God! Mercy!
MSG.7:
God, my Lord, Strong Savior,
protect me when the fighting breaks out!
MSG.8:
Don't let the wicked have their way, God,
don't give them an inch!"
MSG.9:
These troublemakers all around me—
let them drown in their own verbal poison.
MSG.10:
Let God pile hellfire on them,
let him bury them alive in crevasses!
MSG.11:
These loudmouths—
don't let them be taken seriously;
These savages—
let the Devil hunt them down!
MSG.12:
I know that you, God, are on the side of victims,
that you care for the rights of the poor.
MSG.13:
And I know that the righteous personally thank you,
that good people are secure in your presence.

NIV.1:
Psalm 140
For the director of music. A psalm of David.
Rescue me, O Lord, from evil men;
protect me from men of violence,
NIV.2:
who devise evil plans in their hearts
and stir up war every day.
NIV.3:
They make their tongues as sharp as a serpent’s;
the poison of vipers is on their lips. Selah
NIV.4:
Keep me, O Lord, from the hands of the wicked;
protect me from men of violence
who plan to trip my feet.
NIV.5:
Proud men have hidden a snare for me;
they have spread out the cords of their net
and have set traps for me along my path. Selah
NIV.6:
O Lord, I say to you, “You are my God.”
Hear, O Lord, my cry for mercy.
NIV.7:
O Sovereign Lord, my strong deliverer,
who shields my head in the day of battle—
NIV.8:
do not grant the wicked their desires, O Lord;
do not let their plans succeed,
or they will become proud. Selah
NIV.9:
Let the heads of those who surround me
be covered with the trouble their lips have caused.
NIV.10:
Let burning coals fall upon them;
may they be thrown into the fire,
into miry pits, never to rise.
NIV.11:
Let slanderers not be established in the land;
may disaster hunt down men of violence.
NIV.12:
I know that the Lord secures justice for the poor
and upholds the cause of the needy.
NIV.13:
Surely the righteous will praise your name
and the upright will live before you.

NRSVCE.1:
Deliver me, O LORD, from evildoers;
protect me from those who are violent,
NRSVCE.2:
who plan evil things in their minds
and stir up wars continually.
NRSVCE.3:
They make their tongue sharp as a snake’s,
and under their lips is the venom of vipers. Selah
NRSVCE.4:
Guard me, O LORD, from the hands of the wicked;
protect me from the violent
who have planned my downfall.
NRSVCE.5:
The arrogant have hidden a trap for me,
and with cords they have spread a net,
along the road they have set snares for me. Selah
NRSVCE.6:
I say to the LORD, “You are my God;
give ear, O LORD, to the voice of my supplications.”
NRSVCE.7:
O LORD, my Lord, my strong deliverer,
you have covered my head in the day of battle.
NRSVCE.8:
Do not grant, O LORD, the desires of the wicked;
do not further their evil plot. Selah
NRSVCE.9:
Those who surround me lift up their heads;
let the mischief of their lips overwhelm them!
NRSVCE.10:
Let burning coals fall on them!
Let them be flung into pits, no more to rise!
NRSVCE.11:
Do not let the slanderer be established in the land;
let evil speedily hunt down the violent!
NRSVCE.12:
I know that the LORD maintains the cause of the needy,
and executes justice for the poor.
NRSVCE.13:
Surely the righteous shall give thanks to your name;
the upright shall live in your presence.

NET.1: O LORD, rescue me from wicked men! Protect me from violent men,
NET.2: who plan ways to harm me. All day long they stir up conflict.
NET.3: Their tongues wound like a serpent; a viper’s venom is behind their lips. (Selah)
NET.4: O LORD, shelter me from the power of the wicked! Protect me from violent men, who plan to knock me over.
NET.5: Proud men hide a snare for me; evil men spread a net by the path; they set traps for me. (Selah)
NET.6: I say to the LORD, “You are my God.” O LORD, pay attention to my plea for mercy!
NET.7: O sovereign LORD, my strong deliverer, you shield my head in the day of battle.
NET.8: O LORD, do not let the wicked have their way! Do not allow their plan to succeed when they attack! (Selah)
NET.9: As for the heads of those who surround me — may the harm done by their lips overwhelm them!
NET.10: May he rain down fiery coals upon them! May he throw them into the fire! From bottomless pits they will not escape.
NET.11: A slanderer will not endure on the earth; calamity will hunt down a violent man and strike him down.
NET.12: I know that the LORD defends the cause of the oppressed and vindicates the poor.
NET.13: Certainly the godly will give thanks to your name; the morally upright will live in your presence.
